# Introducing the 2025 WIN Partner Index | Wiz Blog

Every cloud security team reaches a moment where the question isn’t,
“Do we need more tools?”
but “
Which ones actually work for our cloud security model?”.
As environments grow and teams scale, integrations become the connective tissue of day-to-day security - showing their value with how well they fit into real workflows, share context, and help teams act faster and with confidence. That’s why modern cloud security depends on an open operating model, where integrations bring their best capabilities together and become a core part of how security gets done.
It’s also why we’re introducing the first annual WIN Partner Index. The WIN Partner Index is a data-backed benchmark of how organizations are actually running cloud security programs today. Built from anonymized and aggregated customer
integration usage data
across the
240+ integrations
in the
Wiz Integration Network (WIN)
, it highlights which integrations organizations rely on most, which are gaining traction fastest, and where teams are seeing consistent value.
As the cloud security industry becomes more complex and the ecosystem of
security tools
grows, understanding how integrations perform in practice becomes essential. This annual Index brings clarity to that complexity, offering a real-world view into how an open security operating model shows up across modern cloud environments.
As organizations adopt more tools across their cloud security stack, integrations are essential for turning dispersed signals and context into actionable intelligence. With a growing security ecosystem, determining which integrations deliver the most impact can be challenging. Wiz’s strategy with the Wiz Integration Network (WIN) helps address that challenge, reflecting Wiz's view that effective cloud security relies on collaboration, not silos.
Katie Norton, Research Manager for DevSecOps and Software Supply Chain Security, IDC
Behind the Index: Wiz and WIN Together
From the start, Wiz was built to operate openly, with integrations treated as a core part of the platform. Wiz gives teams a unified, contextual view of risk across cloud environments, connecting vulnerabilities, misconfigurations, identities, data exposure, and attack paths into a single
Security Graph
. This foundation brings critical signals together in one place, helping teams understand risk in context and prioritize what matters most.
The
Wiz Integration Network (WIN)
extends that foundation across the broader security ecosystem. WIN enables Independent Software Vendors (ISVs) to build bi-directional integrations that connect Wiz with the tools security, development, and cloud teams already use. Wiz risk findings flow into existing workflows, and partner data feeds back into Wiz’s Security Graph, combining security signals into context that drives clearer prioritization and faster remediation, and enables teams to use the power of connected intelligence together, in one view.
Top Insights from the WIN Partner Index 2025
For our inaugural report, we analyzed how customers are actually adopting and using integrations for their team’s workflows, spanning the full range of WIN use cases. The Index evaluated integrations across the lifecycle of a cloud security program - measuring adoption, growth, and real-world impact, from day-to-day workflows and development pipelines to risk reduction and long-term
program maturity
. Looking across the data, several key themes emerged about how teams were really using integrations to run cloud security in 2025:
Security is Everywhere Work Happens:
Cloud security
no longer lives in a single tool or single team. The most adopted integrations stretch across collaboration platforms, development tools, and communication apps, reflecting how work actually gets done. By meeting teams where they collaborate and build, security becomes easier to act on, more scalable across the organization, and accessible without expecting everyone to be a security expert.
Modern Cloud Challenges Demand Modern Approaches:
As cloud environments grow more complex and external surfaces expand, teams are gravitating toward integrations built for today’s realities. The integrations gaining traction bring smarter analysis, broader visibility, and a new way to connect the dots—helping teams stay ahead of the everchanging threat landscape.
Security Lives where Code Lives:
Security isn’t about just shifting left anymore, it’s about starting left. From commit to production, security stays embedded throughout the
SDLC
, delivering real-time context and insight as software ships. The rise of CI/CD and version control integrations makes one thing clear: modern teams expect security to move at developer speed - always on, always relevant, never in the way.
A First Look at the WIN 2025 Leaders
To give a glimpse into the findings, here’s a sneak peek at the WIN 2025 leaders. These partners stood out across the Index’s six categories, including the most widely adopted integrations, the fastest-growing, newly launched standouts, leaders in product innovation and customer education, and this year’s special edition category highlighting popular code tools.
WIN 2025 Leaders
Across all categories, these integrations highlight several clear trends shaping modern cloud security: they
meet teams where they work
, connecting across multiple apps and workflows; they
embed security into development and operational processes
, making it a natural part of how teams build and ship products; and they
deliver visibility and actionable insights
, enabling security teams to prioritize risk and act with confidence. In short, these integrations show how thoughtful design, adoption, and operational alignment can turn tools into essential components of a modern, open cloud security ecosystem.
See how these integrations, and other featured integrations across all Index categories, are essential to a modern cloud security ecosystem: download the
complete Wiz Partner Index 2025 report
and
request a demo
to see them in action.
